hypothesis: If increasing the encoder size (1B to 8B) and decoder depth (2 to 8 layers) reduces the CIE76 ΔE between target colors and colors predicted by transfer-matrix simulations for both training-paraphrase and validation prompts across viewing angles, then higher model capacity improves linguistic-to-physical design accuracy in CHROMA because it enhances generalization to paraphrased prompts and novel optical targets.
